title: Useful contact details for the 2023 Disability Survey | Stats NZ
content:
## Useful contact details for the 2023 Disability Survey
05 September 2023, 2:13pm
If you would like to speak to someone about feelings, questions, or thoughts raised by the 2023 Disability Survey, please contact the following services:
The collection period for the 2023 Disability Survey closed on 26 November 2023.
For more information or if you have any questions about the 2023 Disability Survey, please:
- email survey@stats.govt.nz .

## 24/7 Counselling, support or health advice
24/7 free access to trained counsellors Free phone or text: 1737 Web: www.1737.org.nz
24/7 crisis help line for people experiencing loneliness, depression, despair or distress Free phone: 0800 72 66 66 Web: www.samaritans.org.nz
Free health advice and information Free phone: 0800 611 116 Web: www.healthline.govt.nz

## For children and young people
Support, counselling, and advice for children and teenagers Free phone: 0800 942 8787 (Mon-Sun 11am-11pm) Web: www.whatsup.co.nz
Support, counselling, and advice for young people Free phone: 0800 376 633 Free text: 234 Web: www.youthline.co.nz
Advice about children and young people’s rights Free phone: 0800 224 453 Web: www.manamokopuna.org.nz/help-and-advice

## For parents, whanāu, and carers
Free resources, support, and advice for family carers Free phone: 0800 777 797 Web: www.carers.net.nz
A support and information network run by carers, who look after young people with complex needs. Free phone: 0800 852 693 Web: www.complexcaregroup.org.nz
Parent support and networking, sibling support, and access to disability information. Free phone: 0508 236 236 Web: www.parent2parent.org.nz

## Other helpful services
Expert information and support services in response to older people’s needs Free phone: 0800 652 105 Web: www.ageconcern.org.nz

### Citizens Advice Bureau
Provides information on a wide range of topics, including free budgeting and legal advice Free phone: 0800 367 222 Web: www.cab.org.nz

### Health and Disability Commission (HDC)
Information and advice about your rights when receiving a health or disability service Free phone: 0800 11 22 33 Web: www.hdc.org.nz

### Health & Disability Advocacy Service
Advice and support to help you resolve issues with health and disability services Free phone: 0800 555 050 Web: www.advocacy.org.nz
Free help with day-to-day money matters like budgeting and debt management Free phone: 0800 345 123 Web: www.moneytalks.co.nz
- About the 2023 Household Disability Survey 05 July 2023
- Alternate formats for information about the 2023 Disability Survey 28 July 2023
